mission.title: containerd: Support user namespaces phase I in Kubernetes 1.25+
mission.description: Support user namespaces phase I in Kubernetes 1.25+. Requested by 10+ users.

steps.title: Check current containerd deployment
steps.description:
Verify your containerd version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
helm list -n containerd 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working containerd installation.

steps.title: Review containerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant containerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl get configmap -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=containerd
```
### What is the problem you're trying to solve

Kubernetes 1.25 will have [support for user namespaces][kep-issue], the [phase I of the KEP][kep-phase1] will be implemented and I'm planning to write the containerd patches for that too (I have an

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support user namespaces phase I in Kubernetes 1.25+
steps.description:
This version contains the CRI changes for user namespaces support.
Future patches will use the new fields in the CRI.

Updating the module without using the new fields doesn't cause any
behaviour change.

Updates: #7063

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Upgrade containerd to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade containerd:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade containerd containerd/containerd --namespace containerd
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd
helm list -n containerd
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n containerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=containerd
kubectl get events -n containerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support user namespaces phase I in Kubernetes 1.25+" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This version contains the CRI changes for user namespaces support.
Future patches will use the new fields in the CRI.

Updating the module without using the new fields doesn't cause any
behaviour change.

Updates: #7063

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with containerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
